title: Stelis maguirei
taxonomy: Stelis maguirei is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Stelis is a subgenus within the larger genus Pleurothallis, which is a group of epiphytic orchids. The Stelis genus is characterized by its small, delicate flowers and is native to the tropical regions of the Americas. Stelis maguirei is a specific species within this genus, named after the American botanist Martha Maguire.

intro: Stelis maguirei, also known as Maguire's Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis maguirei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Stelis maguirei is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate inflammation.
